tools.name: search
tools.description: Search GetMyHotels for hotels matching a query. Returns a list of results with ids that can be passed to `fetch` for full details. Provide the destination and travel dates in the query.

tools.name: fetch
tools.description: Fetch the full record for a result id returned by `search` (a hotel) or for a booking reference (prefixed 'bk_'). Returns a document with id, title, text, url, and metadata.

tools.name: search_hotels
tools.description: Search live hotel availability for a destination and date range. Returns a price-sorted list of hotels (JSON in the text), each with a `ref` and its cheapest bookable room's `supplierRoomId`. To book: pass a room's `supplierRoomId` + the hotel `ref` to `get_checkout_quote` then `create_checkout`. Fo

tools.name: get_rooms
tools.description: List the bookable rooms for ONE hotel and date range. Each room's `supplierRoomId` is what you pass to `get_checkout_quote` / `create_checkout`. Use the hotel `ref` from `search_hotels` or `search`. Call this to see all of a chosen hotel's rooms, or to refresh availability before checkout.

tools.name: get_hotel_details
tools.description: Get full property details (description, amenities, photos, policies, location) for a hotel, identified by its `ref` from `search_hotels` (or its supplierCode + supplierHotelId).

tools.name: get_checkout_quote
tools.description: Re-price a specific room live against the supplier before checkout (no booking, no charge). Use the `supplierRoomId` and price/currency from `search_hotels`. Returns the authoritative grand total, a `rateValidUntil` timestamp, and any taxes/fees.

tools.name: create_checkout
tools.description: Create a secure checkout for a chosen hotel room and return a getmyhotels.com URL where the guest completes payment. This does NOT charge the card — the guest pays on the hosted page. Confirm the hotel, room, dates, and price with the user before calling. Call `get_checkout_quote` first to confirm t

tools.name: get_booking
tools.description: Look up a hotel booking by its management token (sent in the confirmation email). Returns the booking status, hotel, dates, and guest details.

tools.name: list_trips
tools.description: List the signed-in user's saved trips (itineraries).

tools.name: save_trip
tools.description: Create a new saved trip (itinerary) for the signed-in user.

tools.name: add_trip_item
tools.description: Add an item (hotel, flight, activity, etc.) to a saved trip on a given day.

tools.name: list_my_bookings
tools.description: List the signed-in user's hotel bookings.

tools.name: get_my_booking
tools.description: Get full details of one of the signed-in user's bookings by its public id.

tools.name: request_booking_action_code
tools.description: Email a 6-digit verification code to the booking owner, required before cancelling or modifying a booking. Step 1 of 2 — then call `cancel_booking`/`modify_booking` with the code.

tools.name: cancel_booking
tools.description: Cancel one of the signed-in user's bookings. Requires the 6-digit `verificationCode` from `request_booking_action_code`. This may be irreversible and can trigger a refund per policy.

tools.name: modify_booking
tools.description: Change one of the signed-in user's bookings. Requires the 6-digit `verificationCode` from `request_booking_action_code`. Special-request edits always apply. A DATE change is pushed to the hotel supplier as a cancel-and-rebook at current availability, so it only works for suppliers that support amend

tools.name: list_payment_methods
tools.description: List the signed-in user's saved payment cards (brand, last 4, expiry, default). Use the returned `publicId` with `create_one_click_checkout` to pre-select a card.

tools.name: create_one_click_checkout
tools.description: Create a checkout for the signed-in user with a saved card pre-selected, and return a getmyhotels.com URL where they confirm + pay in one click. Does NOT charge the card — the user confirms on the hosted page. Get the `savedPaymentMethodPublicId` from `list_payment_methods`. Confirm the hotel, room,

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "search" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "fetch" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "create_checkout" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Create a secure checkout for a chosen hotel room and return a getmyhotels.com URL where the guest completes payment. This does NOT charge the card — the guest p…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "modify_booking" stacks urgency/override words (always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Change one of the signed-in user's bookings. Requires the 6-digit `verificationCode` from `request_booking_action_code`. Special-request edits always apply. A D…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp.getmyhotels.com" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: fetch, get_booking, create_one_click_checkout · Private data: fetch, get_booking · Public sink: get_booking, request_booking_action_code · Destructive: modify_booking.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: fetch, get_booking, create_one_click_checkout · Private data: fetch, get_booking · Public sink: get_booking, request_booking_action_code · Destructive: modify_booking

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
